english: Prices are insane these days. Even grocery shopping is getting expensive.
alt: Everything's so pricey now. The cost of living is no joke.
note: cost of living = 생활비 / insane = 미친 듯한

korean: 금리가 또 올랐대 대출 이자 어떡하지
english: Interest rates went up again. What am I gonna do about my loan payments?
alt: They raised the rates again. My mortgage is killing me.
note: interest rate = 금리 / mortgage = 주택담보대출, loan payment = 대출 상환금

english: The stock market is a total rollercoaster these days.
alt: Stocks have been all over the place lately.
note: rollercoaster = 급등락을 반복하다 / all over the place = 오락가락하다

korean: 집값이 너무 올라서 내 집 마련은 꿈도 못 꿔
english: Housing prices are so high that owning a home is just a dream.
alt: Real estate is so expensive I can't even dream of buying a place.
note: owning a home = 내 집 마련 / real estate = 부동산

korean: 환율이 이렇게 높으니까 해외여행 엄두도 못 내겠어
english: With the exchange rate this high I can't even think about traveling abroad.
alt: The exchange rate is so bad that overseas trips are out of the question.
note: exchange rate = 환율 / out of the question = 불가능한

korean: 요즘 경기 침체라고 하더라 취업이 더 힘들어지겠다
english: They say we're in a recession now. Job hunting is gonna get even harder.
alt: The economy is in a downturn. Finding a job will be tougher.
note: recession = 경기 침체 / downturn = 하락세

korean: 인플레이션이 심해서 월급은 그대로인데 쓸 돈은 늘었어
english: Inflation is so bad that my salary stays the same, but my expenses keep going up.
alt: With inflation this high, my paycheck doesn't stretch like it used to.
note: inflation = 인플레이션 / paycheck doesn't stretch = 월급이 부족하다

korean: 부동산 거품 꺼지면 큰일 나는 거 아니야?
english: Isn't it gonna be a disaster if the real estate bubble bursts?
alt: What happens when the housing bubble pops? That's scary.
note: bubble bursts = 거품이 꺼지다 / disaster = 큰일

korean: 요즘 코인 투자하는 사람 진짜 많더라
english: So many people are investing in crypto these days.
alt: Everyone and their mom is into cryptocurrency now.
note: crypto/cryptocurrency = 암호화폐 / investing = 투자하다

korean: 물가 오르는 속도를 월급이 못 따라가
english: Wages can't keep up with how fast prices are rising.
alt: Salary increases can't match the rate of inflation.
note: keep up with = ~를 따라가다 / rate of inflation = 물가 상승률

korean: 전세 사기 뉴스 보면 진짜 무서워
english: The news about deposit fraud scams is really scary.
alt: All these rental deposit scams are terrifying.
note: deposit fraud = 전세 사기 / scam = 사기

korean: 금리 인하 기대했는데 아직 멀었나 봐
english: I was hoping for a rate cut but it seems like it's still far off.
alt: Looks like the interest rate cut we were waiting for isn't coming anytime soon.
note: rate cut = 금리 인하 / far off = 아직 멀다

english: Some people are even talking about stagflation.
alt: There's talk of stagflation hitting us.
note: stagflation = 스태그플레이션(경기침체+물가상승) / hitting us = 닥치다

english: Small business owners must be having such a hard time these days.
alt: I feel for the self-employed folks right now. Times are tough.
note: small business owner = 자영업자 / times are tough = 시기가 힘들다

korean: 미국 연준이 어떻게 하느냐에 따라 우리 경제도 흔들리잖아
english: Our economy shakes depending on what the Fed does, you know.
alt: The Korean economy is so tied to whatever the US Federal Reserve decides.
note: the Fed = 미국 연방준비제도 / tied to = ~에 연동되다

korean: 적금 이율이 너무 낮아서 돈 모으기 힘들어
english: Savings account rates are so low it's hard to save money.
alt: The interest on savings is pathetic. How are we supposed to save?
note: savings account = 적금 / pathetic = 한심할 정도로 낮은

english: I heard the trade deficit is getting serious.
alt: The trade deficit has been really bad lately.
note: trade deficit = 무역 적자 / serious = 심각한

english: Did you see the news that the unemployment rate is going up?
alt: Have you heard unemployment is on the rise?
note: unemployment rate = 실업률 / on the rise = 상승 중인

korean: 세금은 올리고 복지는 줄이면 누가 좋아하겠어
english: If they raise taxes and cut welfare, who's gonna be happy about that?
alt: Raising taxes while cutting benefits? Nobody's going to support that.
note: raise taxes = 세금을 올리다 / cut welfare = 복지를 줄이다

korean: 공급망 문제 때문에 물건값이 다 올랐어
english: Prices went up across the board because of supply chain issues.
alt: Supply chain disruptions drove up the cost of everything.
note: supply chain = 공급망 / across the board = 전반적으로

korean: 중산층이 점점 줄어들고 있다는 게 체감돼
english: You can really feel that the middle class is shrinking.
alt: It's obvious that the middle class is disappearing.
note: middle class = 중산층 / shrinking = 줄어들다

english: Household debt hit a record high. That's worrying.
alt: They say household debt is at an all-time high. That's concerning.
note: household debt = 가계부채 / record high = 사상 최고치

english: More people are getting by on convenience store lunch boxes.
alt: The number of people living off convenience store meals is rising.
note: get by on = ~로 때우다 / living off = ~에 의존하다

english: Even delivery fees went up so ordering food is a burden too.
alt: Delivery charges are so high now that ordering in feels like a luxury.
note: delivery fee = 배달비 / burden = 부담

korean: 부자는 더 부자 되고 가난한 사람은 더 힘들어져
english: The rich get richer, and the poor have it even harder.
alt: It's the same old story — the wealthy thrive while the poor struggle.
note: the rich get richer = 부익부 / thrive = 번성하다

korean: 1인 가구가 많아지면서 소비 패턴도 바뀌고 있대
english: Consumer patterns are changing as single-person households increase.
alt: Spending habits are shifting with the rise of people living alone.
note: single-person household = 1인 가구 / consumer pattern = 소비 패턴

korean: 은퇴 후 생활비 걱정이 제일 커
english: My biggest worry about retirement is living expenses.
alt: The cost of living after retirement is my number one concern.
note: retirement = 은퇴 / living expenses = 생활비

korean: 연금 받을 수 있긴 한 거야 우리 세대는?
english: Will our generation even get to receive a pension?
alt: Are we actually going to see any pension money when we retire?
note: pension = 연금 / generation = 세대

english: I heard the consumer price index went up again.
alt: The CPI rose again apparently.
note: consumer price index (CPI) = 소비자 물가 지수

english: It's a tough world without a side hustle these days.
alt: You practically need a side gig to survive nowadays.
note: side hustle/side gig = 부업 / tough world = 힘든 세상

english: The central bank printed too much money and that's the problem.
alt: The issue is the central bank flooded the market with cash.
note: central bank = 중앙은행 / printed money = 돈을 풀다

korean: 글로벌 경기 둔화가 우리한테도 영향을 미치겠지
english: The global economic slowdown will probably affect us too.
alt: We'll definitely feel the impact of the worldwide recession.
note: economic slowdown = 경기 둔화 / affect = 영향을 미치다

english: Oil prices are up so gas prices are no joke.
alt: Fuel costs are through the roof because crude oil prices spiked.
note: crude oil = 원유 / through the roof = 하늘을 찌르다

english: This presidential election is really intense.
alt: The race for president is incredibly competitive this time.
note: presidential election = 대선 / intense = 치열한

korean: 투표는 꼭 해야지 안 하면 불평할 자격도 없어
english: You have to vote. If you don't, you have no right to complain.
alt: Make sure you vote. You can't complain if you don't show up.
note: vote = 투표하다 / no right to complain = 불평할 자격이 없다

english: The ruling party and the opposition are always fighting, that's all they do.
alt: All the majority and minority parties do is argue with each other.
note: ruling party = 여당 / opposition = 야당

english: The campaign promises sound grand, but can they actually keep them?
alt: Big promises during the election, but will they follow through?
note: campaign promise = 공약 / follow through = 이행하다

english: They passed another bill in the National Assembly.
alt: The parliament approved a new law again.
note: bill = 법안 / National Assembly = 국회

english: Politicians are all talk and no action.
alt: Politicians make fancy speeches but never deliver.
note: all talk and no action = 말만 하고 행동은 없다 / deliver = 실행하다

korean: 이번 정책은 좀 현실적인 것 같아
english: This policy actually seems realistic.
alt: I think this policy is grounded in reality for once.
note: policy = 정책 / realistic = 현실적인

english: Have you signed any national petitions?
alt: Did you ever participate in a public petition?
note: national petition = 국민 청원 / sign = 서명하다

korean: 세금 낭비하는 거 보면 진짜 열 받아
english: It really makes me angry to see tax money being wasted.
alt: Seeing taxpayer money go to waste is infuriating.
note: tax money = 세금 / wasted = 낭비되다 / infuriating = 분노하게 하는

english: I was shocked when I saw the impeachment news.
alt: The impeachment headlines caught me completely off guard.
note: impeachment = 탄핵 / caught off guard = 놀라다

korean: 정치에 관심 없다는 게 사치인 시대야
english: In this day and age, being apolitical is a luxury.
alt: Not caring about politics is a privilege these days.
note: apolitical = 정치에 무관심한 / privilege = 사치/특권

english: Poll results change every day so I have no idea what to expect.
alt: The survey numbers flip-flop daily. It's hard to predict.
note: poll = 여론조사 / flip-flop = 오락가락하다

english: Local elections don't get as much attention.
alt: People tend to care less about regional elections.
note: local election = 지방선거 / attention = 관심

korean: 국회의원들 세비가 너무 많은 거 아니야?
english: Don't you think lawmakers' salaries are way too high?
alt: Isn't the pay for members of parliament excessive?
note: lawmaker = 국회의원 / salary = 세비 / excessive = 과도한

english: What matters is where the welfare budget gets spent.
alt: It's all about how they allocate the social welfare funds.
note: welfare budget = 복지 예산 / allocate = 배분하다

english: The president's approval rating keeps dropping.
alt: Presidential approval is on a downward trend.
note: approval rating = 지지율 / downward trend = 하락세

korean: 정당 간 협력이 필요한 시기인데 왜 이러는지
english: It's a time when bipartisan cooperation is needed. Why are they acting like this?
alt: This is when parties should work together but they just keep bickering.
note: bipartisan cooperation = 초당적 협력 / bickering = 다투다

korean: 선거 때만 되면 길에 현수막이 넘쳐나
english: Election banners are everywhere on the streets when elections come around.
alt: The streets get flooded with campaign banners every election season.
note: election banner = 선거 현수막 / flooded with = 넘쳐나다

korean: 후보자 토론 봤어? 누가 잘한 것 같아?
english: Did you watch the candidates' debate? Who do you think did better?
alt: Did you catch the debate? Who won in your opinion?
note: debate = 토론 / candidate = 후보자

korean: 정치 뉴스 볼 때마다 한숨만 나와
english: Every time I watch political news I just sigh.
alt: Political news always makes me sigh these days.
note: sigh = 한숨 쉬다

english: Did you do early voting? The lines were crazy long.
alt: I went to vote early. The queue was insane.
note: early voting = 사전투표 / queue = 줄

english: Did you see the news that the minister got dismissed?
alt: Heard they fired that cabinet minister?
note: minister = 장관 / dismissed/fired = 경질되다

korean: 정치 성향 때문에 친구랑 싸운 적 있어?
english: Have you ever fought with a friend over political views?
alt: Did you and your friend ever clash because of your political stance?
note: political views/stance = 정치 성향 / clash = 충돌하다

korean: 그 법안 통과되면 우리한테 직접 영향 있어
english: If that bill passes, it'll directly affect us.
alt: That legislation would have a direct impact on us if it goes through.
note: bill/legislation = 법안 / direct impact = 직접적 영향

korean: 국민 세금으로 그걸 한다고? 말이 돼?
english: They're using taxpayer money for that? Does that even make sense?
alt: Spending public funds on that? That's ridiculous.
note: taxpayer money = 국민 세금 / make sense = 말이 되다

korean: 선거 공약 이행률이 얼마나 되는지 궁금해
english: I wonder what percentage of election promises actually get fulfilled.
alt: I'm curious about the follow-through rate on campaign pledges.
note: fulfillment rate = 이행률 / campaign pledge = 선거 공약

english: Political reform is urgently needed.
alt: We need political reform and we need it now.
note: political reform = 정치 개혁 / urgently = 시급하게

english: What do you think about the proportional representation system?
alt: How do you feel about proportional representation?
note: proportional representation = 비례대표제

english: I'm surprised there were so many invalid ballots.
alt: The number of spoiled votes was shocking.
note: invalid ballot = 무효표 / spoiled vote = 무효표

korean: 야당이 반대만 하는 건 아닌지 모르겠어
english: I'm not sure if the opposition is just opposing everything for the sake of it.
alt: Is the opposition party just blocking everything without any alternatives?
note: opposition = 야당 / for the sake of it = 그냥 반대를 위한

english: What's gonna change with this cabinet reshuffle?
alt: Will the cabinet shake-up actually make any difference?
note: cabinet reshuffle = 개각 / make a difference = 달라지다

english: Everyone's been talking about carbon neutrality lately.
alt: Carbon neutrality is a big topic these days.
note: carbon neutrality = 탄소중립

english: I can feel climate change. Summers keep getting hotter every year.
alt: Climate change is real. Each summer is worse than the last.
note: climate change = 기후변화 / getting hotter = 더워지다

korean: 분리수거 제대로 하는 사람이 얼마나 될까
english: I wonder how many people actually recycle properly.
alt: Do people really sort their recycling correctly?
note: recycle/sort recycling = 분리수거하다

korean: 전기차 보급이 빨라지고 있긴 한데 충전소가 부족해
english: Electric vehicles are spreading fast, but there aren't enough charging stations.
alt: EV adoption is growing but the charging infrastructure can't keep up.
note: electric vehicle (EV) = 전기차 / charging station = 충전소

english: Natural disasters are becoming more and more frequent.
alt: We're seeing natural disasters happen more often than ever.
note: natural disaster = 자연재해 / frequent = 빈번한

english: Are you participating in the movement to reduce single-use items?
alt: Have you been cutting back on disposable products?
note: single-use items = 일회용품 / cut back on = 줄이다

english: The fine dust is so bad I'm scared to go outside.
alt: I don't want to go out because of the air pollution.
note: fine dust = 미세먼지 / air pollution = 대기오염

korean: 태양광 에너지가 대세가 될 거라고 생각해?
english: Do you think solar energy will become the mainstream?
alt: Is solar power really going to be the future?
note: solar energy = 태양광 에너지 / mainstream = 대세

english: Plastic pollution is serious. The ocean is basically a landfill.
alt: Plastic waste is out of control. Our oceans are turning into garbage dumps.
note: plastic pollution = 플라스틱 오염 / landfill = 쓰레기 매립지

english: I heard the damage from the heavy rain was massive.
alt: The flooding from the downpour was devastating apparently.
note: heavy rain = 폭우 / devastating = 엄청난 피해를 주는

english: Opinions are divided on restarting nuclear power plants.
alt: People are split on bringing nuclear plants back online.
note: nuclear power plant = 원전 / divided = 나뉘다

english: Do you think we should introduce a carbon tax?
alt: Should the government implement a carbon tax?
note: carbon tax = 탄소세 / implement = 도입하다

english: Too many companies are greenwashing.
alt: So many businesses just pretend to be eco-friendly.
note: greenwashing = 그린워싱(위장 환경주의) / eco-friendly = 친환경적인

english: Island nations are in danger because of rising sea levels.
alt: Rising sea levels are threatening to swallow small island countries.
note: rising sea levels = 해수면 상승 / island nations = 섬나라

korean: 에코백 쓴다고 환경이 살아나는 건 아니지
english: Using an eco bag alone isn't going to save the environment.
alt: Carrying a tote bag doesn't automatically make you environmentally conscious.
note: eco bag = 에코백 / environmentally conscious = 환경 의식이 있는

korean: 산불이 매년 더 심해지는 것 같아
english: Wildfires seem to be getting worse every year.
alt: Forest fires are becoming more severe annually.
note: wildfire = 산불 / severe = 심각한

korean: 재생에너지 비중을 늘려야 하는 거 맞지?
english: We should increase the share of renewable energy right?
alt: Shouldn't we be expanding our use of renewable energy?
note: renewable energy = 재생에너지 / share/proportion = 비중

english: Extreme weather has become the new normal.
alt: Abnormal climate patterns are just everyday life now.
note: extreme weather = 이상기후 / new normal = 새로운 일상

english: Food waste is actually a major cause of environmental pollution.
alt: Apparently food waste contributes significantly to pollution.
note: food waste = 음식물 쓰레기 / contribute to = ~에 기여하다

korean: 전기료 폭탄 맞을까 봐 에어컨 못 틀겠어
english: I'm afraid of getting hit with a huge electricity bill so I can't turn on the AC.
alt: I don't dare use the air conditioner because I'm worried about the electric bill.
note: electricity bill = 전기료 / huge bill = 폭탄 요금

english: Companies say they're doing ESG management, but is it for real?
alt: All these corporations claim to follow ESG principles but I'm skeptical.
note: ESG = 환경·사회·지배구조 / skeptical = 회의적인

english: The drought is so severe that crop damage is extensive.
alt: Farming communities are suffering heavy losses due to the drought.
note: drought = 가뭄 / crop damage = 농작물 피해

korean: 제로 웨이스트 생활 실천하기 쉽지 않아
english: Living a zero-waste lifestyle isn't easy to practice.
alt: Zero-waste living is easier said than done.
note: zero waste = 제로 웨이스트 / easier said than done = 말이 쉽지

english: Glaciers are melting faster than expected.
alt: The rate of glacier melt is exceeding predictions.
note: glacier = 빙하 / exceeding predictions = 예상을 초과하다

korean: 환경 문제는 개인 노력만으론 한계가 있어
english: Individual effort alone has its limits when it comes to environmental issues.
alt: Personal action isn't enough to solve environmental problems.
note: individual effort = 개인 노력 / limits = 한계

english: There have been deaths from the heat wave recently.
alt: People have actually died from the extreme heat lately.
note: heat wave = 폭염 / deaths = 사망자

korean: 생태계가 무너지고 있다는 전문가 말이 무섭다
english: It's scary when experts say the ecosystem is collapsing.
alt: Environmental scientists warning about ecosystem collapse is terrifying.
note: ecosystem = 생태계 / collapsing = 무너지다

english: Does the hydrogen car have a brighter future than the electric car?
alt: Will hydrogen vehicles outshine EVs in the long run?
note: hydrogen car = 수소차 / outshine = 능가하다

korean: 멸종위기 동물 뉴스 보면 마음이 아파
english: It breaks my heart to see news about endangered species.
alt: Hearing about endangered animals always makes me sad.
note: endangered species = 멸종위기종 / breaks my heart = 마음이 아프다

korean: 우리 세대가 환경 문제를 해결 못 하면 다음 세대가 고생해
english: If our generation can't solve environmental issues, the next generation will suffer.
alt: If we don't fix the environment now, our kids will pay the price.
note: next generation = 다음 세대 / pay the price = 대가를 치르다

korean: ChatGPT 나오고 나서 세상이 완전 바뀌었어
english: The world completely changed after ChatGPT came out.
alt: Everything's been different since ChatGPT hit the scene.
note: hit the scene = 등장하다

korean: AI가 내 일자리를 대체할까 봐 걱정돼
english: I'm worried AI might replace my job.
alt: I'm concerned about AI taking over my career.
note: replace = 대체하다 / take over = 차지하다

korean: 자율주행차 사고 뉴스 보면 아직 멀었나 싶어
english: When I see self-driving car accident news, I think we're still far from ready.
alt: Autonomous vehicle crashes make me doubt the technology.
note: self-driving car = 자율주행차 / autonomous vehicle = 자율주행 차량

english: Personal data breaches happen way too often.
alt: There are way too many data leak incidents.
note: data breach = 개인정보 유출 / incident = 사건

korean: 메타버스가 진짜 대세가 될 수 있을까?
english: Can the metaverse really become mainstream?
alt: Is the metaverse actually going to take off?
note: metaverse = 메타버스 / take off = 인기를 얻다

english: It's a scary world with deepfake technology.
